threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube.' Everyone else just refers to it as 'the Overground' when you're in Hackney.
Heard a guy on the 133 bragging about swiping his mate's seat, only to sit on an old lady's lap after forgetting it’s not rush hour. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home was when I found a spontaneous dance party in a courtyard, complete with an old man playing the accordion. Only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you spot techno parties in abandoned subway stations at dawn. The echoes of bass and laughter linger long after the rave ends.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only a true local knows to avoid 16th Street BART on Fridays; the echo of broken brakes and bus delays is like clockwork.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 21 bus this morning; it was packed, and a guy in a vintage cape was reading Shakespeare out loud. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all sip vermut daily, but most locals save it for Sunday aperitifs with family, not a routine.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by the way they dodge the locals at the market, still browsing like it's a museum instead of a place to haggle.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when you hear the 38 bus driver shout, 'How many times do I have to say it?' It's a rite of passage to just roll with it.
Caught a glimpse of a couple arguing over a spilled drink on the 68 last Saturday. She was fuming, but he just kept laughing. Classic Brixton chaos.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I stepped out to the smell of fresh falafel and heard a street musician playing a quirky cover of 'Wonderwall' on an accordion.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: In Kreuzberg, you can spot a Berliner casually sipping coffee while waiting for a keelboat race on the canal – total vibe only locals get.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they can name three taco trucks on Mission Street and know their schedules by heart, even the ones who disappear at midnight.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a tux trying to calm down a dog that bolted on the Civic Center tracks. Only in SF do we get fancy and furry drama in one breath.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think 'tapas' means a plate of Olives everywhere. Most of us prefer a good bocadillo over the tourist traps, any day.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones standing confused at the wrong bus stop, still clutching their map like it’s a lifeline. Classic rookie move.
